skill.name: deployment-pipeline-design
skill.description: Design multi-stage CI/CD pipelines with approval gates, security checks, and deployment orchestration. Use when architecting deployment workflows, setting up continuous delivery, or implementing GitOps.
skill.summary: Design multi-stage CI/CD pipelines with approval gates, security checks, and deployment orchestration
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is pure documentation with YAML/Kubernetes configuration examples. No actual code execution, network requests, or credential access occurs - only documentation patterns for CI/CD pipeline design.

medium_findings.title: Documentation Pattern Detection
medium_findings.description: Static scanner detected YAML syntax in code blocks as potential shell commands. This is a false positive - the file contains documentation with example YAML configurations, not executable code.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All instances are YAML/Kubernetes examples within code fences (```yaml, ```bash, ```python). These are documentation examples, not actual executable code.

content.value_statement: This skill helps DevOps engineers design robust multi-stage CI/CD pipelines with approval gates, security scanning, and automated deployment strategies for reliable software releases.
actual_capabilities: Design multi-stage CI/CD pipeline architectures with proper stage ordering
Implement approval gate patterns for manual and automated workflows
Configure deployment strategies including rolling, blue-green, and canary
Create pipeline orchestration with parallel execution and dependency management
Set up automated rollback strategies for failed deployments
Define monitoring and metrics integration for deployment verification
limitations: Does not execute actual deployments - provides guidance and templates only
Does not integrate with specific CI/CD platforms (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins)
Does not include secrets management implementation details

use_cases.title: Architect New Deployment Pipeline
use_cases.description: Design a complete CI/CD pipeline from source to production with approval gates and security scanning stages
use_cases.target_user: DevOps engineers building new deployment infrastructure

use_cases.title: Improve Existing Pipeline Reliability
use_cases.description: Add automated rollback, health checks, and monitoring to existing deployment workflows
use_cases.target_user: Site reliability engineers optimizing deployment processes

use_cases.title: Implement Progressive Delivery
use_cases.description: Set up canary or blue-green deployment strategies for safe production releases
use_cases.target_user: Platform engineers implementing safe deployment practices

prompt_templates.title: Basic Pipeline Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a multi-stage CI/CD pipeline with build, test, staging, and production deployment stages. Include approval gates before production.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a new deployment pipeline from scratch

prompt_templates.title: Add Rollback Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Add automated rollback capabilities to our existing pipeline. Include health check verification and automatic rollback on failure.
prompt_templates.scenario: Improving pipeline reliability with automated recovery

prompt_templates.title: Implement Canary Deployment
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a canary deployment strategy for our Kubernetes application. Include traffic shifting percentages and monitoring integration.
prompt_templates.scenario: Implementing progressive delivery for risk mitigation

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Environment Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a pipeline configuration that handles development, staging, and production environments with proper approval workflows for each stage.

output_examples.input: Design a CI/CD pipeline with approval gates
output_examples.output: A comprehensive pipeline design including: Source (checkout) -> Build (compile, package) -> Test (unit, integration, security) -> Staging Deploy -> Integration Tests -> Approval Gate (manual) -> Production Deploy -> Health Check -> Monitoring. Each stage with specific actions and failure handling.

output_examples.input: What are the best deployment strategies?
output_examples.output: The main deployment strategies are: 1) Rolling - gradual rollout with zero downtime, best for most apps; 2) Blue-Green - instant switch between two environments, easy rollback but doubles infrastructure temporarily; 3) Canary - gradual traffic shift to new version, allows real-user testing; 4) Feature Flags - deploy code without releasing, instant rollback at feature level.

best_practices: Use parallel execution for independent pipeline stages to reduce total pipeline duration
Implement fail-fast patterns by running fast tests before slow ones
Add health check verification after each deployment stage before proceeding
Use artifact caching to speed up builds and reduce compute costs
Store secrets in secret management systems (Vault, cloud secret managers) not in pipeline code
anti_patterns: Do not skip security scanning stages to speed up deployments
Do not deploy directly to production without approval gates
Do not skip staging environment testing before production deployment
Do not forget to document pipeline stages and their purpose

faq.question: What is an approval gate in CI/CD?
faq.answer: An approval gate is a manual or automated checkpoint in a deployment pipeline that must be passed before proceeding to the next stage. It ensures that required reviews, tests, or sign-offs occur before production deployments.

faq.question: Which deployment strategy has zero downtime?
faq.answer: Rolling deployment strategy provides zero downtime by gradually replacing old instances with new ones. Blue-green and canary deployments also achieve zero downtime through environment switching or traffic gradual shifting.

faq.question: How do I implement automated rollback?
faq.answer: Automated rollback can be implemented by adding health check verification steps after deployment. If health checks fail, trigger the rollback command (like kubectl rollout undo) to revert to the previous version.

faq.question: What is the difference between canary and blue-green deployment?
faq.answer: Canary deployment gradually shifts a percentage of traffic to the new version, allowing real-user testing. Blue-green deployment runs two identical environments and switches all traffic at once, providing instant rollback but requiring double infrastructure.

faq.question: How do I secure secrets in deployment pipelines?
faq.answer: Never hardcode secrets in pipeline configuration. Use secret management systems like HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, or GitHub Secrets. Reference secrets as environment variables that are injected at runtime.

faq.question: What are the key pipeline metrics to track?
faq.answer: Key metrics include: Deployment Frequency (how often you deploy), Lead Time (commit to production), Change Failure Rate (percentage of failed deployments), and Mean Time to Recovery (MTTR) - time to recover from failures.
